6 min read
Just now

--

Art, Books, Poetry, Music, Movies, AI, and other Things We Cannot Ignore
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Deepak Gupta on UnsplashI received an email notification about the upcoming Medium Day event. When I registered on Zoom, I noticed I’ve signed up to attend the last three years. But this year Medium Day means more to me. It’s a reminder that I have neglected my writing passion far too long.
I like to write stream-of-consciousness, where I am as surprised as the reader of what comes up.
It doesn’t take long for the fear-based side of my brain to kick in with negativity… “Who cares? The world is in survival mode, what could you possibly add to the conversation that isn’t controversial or fodder for keyboard warriors?”
Occasionally, I ride the sea of negativity like the expert mind surfer I’ve become, and make it to the other side to actually get my thoughts written down before I get distracted with the merry-go-round of tasks that fill my day.
This is day three of writing this blog post. So much for spontaneity!
Most of my thoughts never make it beyond my sacred journals. I am a tried and true fan of Morning Pages, thanks to Julia Cameron!
But today, I’m venturing beyond the private page. Believing that those meant to read these words will find them, think about their passions, and what they have been…
